articles.title: Jaisalmer faces water shortages during canal repairs amid heatwave - The News Mill
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Jaisalmer in Rajasthan is experiencing an acute water shortage as canal maintenance work coincides with a severe heatwave affecting Northern, Central, and Western India. Temperatures have consistently exceeded 42°C, exacerbating the scarcity of water and prompting emergency responses from local authorities.
The water crisis has led to widespread dissatisfaction among residents, particularly in informal colonies where municipal tanker water is supplied only once every four to five days. A local resident reported enduring 10 days without water and stated, “No water comes out of the taps. It has been 10 days now without water…We told officers several times and even submitted an application, but nothing happened.”
Rajasthan Public Health Engineering Department (PHED) Executive Engineer Niranjan Meena attributed the disruption to scheduled canal maintenance beginning on May 10. He confirmed that water tankers were deployed daily to seven designated locations within informal (kacchi) colonies under close supervision. Meena also noted a government-backed water supply scheme, supported by the Chief Minister, aimed at extending coverage to unserved areas of the city.
He added, “Work on the canal has been ongoing since 10th May; it was completed yesterday. The water supply to the city resumed yesterday…During this period, water was supposed to be conserved and used. This canal closure was done properly, and there was no issue anywhere. Alternate arrangements for water were made in ‘kacchi’ colonies. Water supply will be properly resumed within 72 hours. As per the directions of the District Collector, water tankers are sent daily to 7 designated locations in special ‘kacchi’ colonies. People are getting water through that. Colonies where the water supply was affected for any reason it is being supplied under continuous monitoring. As per the announcement by the CM, the water supply scheme is going to be initiated in several areas of the city. The colonies which are left out will be included, and in the time to come, arrangements will be made for them too.”
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) recorded a temperature of 42°C in Jaisalmer on Saturday morning. In Patna, Bihar, measures have been taken at the Patna Zoo to provide relief for wildlife affected by the rising heat. Cooling arrangements include the installation of fans and coolers for animals such as lions, bears, tigers, and chimpanzees, while air conditioning units have been set up in the snake house. Water sprinklers are also in operation to mitigate the heat. The animals’ diets have been supplemented with fruits including watermelon, muskmelon, and bananas.
Visitors to the zoo have access to cold water dispensed from earthen pots (ghadas) placed around the park, a provision which has been well received.
Similarly, Odisha is experiencing elevated temperatures and humid conditions, with the IMD forecasting the continuation of heatwaves for the coming days. Dr Manorama Mohanty, Director of IMD Bhubaneswar, stated, “Several areas are experiencing extreme heat, with temperatures reaching 42°C or above at 16 locations and 40°C or above at 24 locations… Heat waves and humid conditions are expected to persist tomorrow as well. This is likely to continue for the next five days. Thunderstorms, lightning, and strong winds are very likely in Odisha districts over the next 24 hours. Hailstorms are also likely in Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Angul, Dhenkanal, Boudh, and Balangir districts. Therefore, an orange alert has been issued for hailstorms and strong winds… People should be aware of the situation. They are advised to avoid prolonged exposure to the sun and to work indoors as much as possible…”
The IMD’s Daily Weather Report and Forecast Bulletin issued on Friday predicted the likelihood of heat wave conditions persisting over the following six days.
